description: This figure contains two scanning electron microscopy images showing surface morphologies of Fe–7.5Mn–4Al–5Cr alloy samples after isothermal oxidation at 800°C in 200 torr pure oxygen. The left image displays a hollow, porous oxide mound formed on the alloy surface after 24 hr of oxidation, with abundant voids distributed across the mound surface. The right image presents the oxidized alloy surface after 100 hr of oxidation, showing an agglomerated, rough bulk oxide cluster, alongside filament-shaped manganese oxide threads located next to regions of spalled alumina scale. These observations verify that the 4 wt% Al content in this alloy is insufficient to form a stable continuous protective alumina scale, and scale degradation progresses with extended oxidation duration. The oxide threads support the conclusion that aluminum depletion of the substrate below the alumina scale induces austenite formation, which promotes the growth of Mn-rich oxide features even in Cr-containing Fe-Mn-Al alloys.
